poster_title: Query-Based Router by Dual Contrastive Learning for Assembling Large Language Models
authors: Shuhao Chen, Weisen Jiang, Baijiong Lin, James T. Kwok, Yu Zhang
paper_info: 38th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2024)

content: LLMs excel in diverse tasks, showing proficiency and versatility.
Public availability of LLMs allows for fine-tuning to generalists or specialists.
Assembling LLMs can leverage complementary strengths, enhancing performance.
Current routing models struggle with queries where multiple LLMs perform well.
ZOOTER's loss function is inadequate for similar LLM performances.
Small score differences between top LLMs lead to unstable training.
RouterDC combines an encoder with learnable LLM embeddings.
Sample-LLM contrastive loss pulls query embedding closer to top-performing LLMs and pushes away from bottom-performing LLMs.
Sample-sample contrastive loss maximizes similarity within groups and minimizes between groups.
Experiments on challenging reasoning tasks: language understanding, code generation, and mathematical reasoning.
Evaluation in both in-distribution and out-of-distribution settings.
RouterDC surpasses top-performing LLMs and existing routing methods on in-distribution (+2.76%) and out-of-distribution (+1.90%) tasks.
Two contrastive losses improve training stability and performance.
Novel framework for learning a router to select appropriate LLMs using dual contrastive learning.
Parameter-efficient (fewer than 100M parameters) and computation-efficient (no gradient backpropagation through LLMs).
Efficient inference (6× faster than Voting) by computing only the selected LLM and router.
RouterDC effectively utilizes the complementary strengths of LLMs, achieving state-of-the-art performance.
Dual contrastive learning framework is both parameter-efficient and computationally efficient, suitable for practical applications.
